notes: Labeled trigger prompts for description routing. Split ~60/40 train/validation. Run each query 3x in a fresh session; pass if trigger rate >= 0.5 when should_trigger, < 0.5 when not. Do not optimize description against validation. Negatives emphasize native break-glass, WSL/MCP shortcuts, skip-evidence, and generic infra.

train.query: List Proxmox remotes through PDM and show JSON inventory
Create a snapshot on VM 101 via the official PDM client
Migrate a QEMU guest between nodes using proxmox-datacenter-manager-client
PDM task returned a UPID — confirm it actually finished OK
From Windows, call the PDM skill SSH bridge to remote list
Troubleshoot PDM login TLS fingerprint / ticket cache
Shut down a guest through Datacenter Manager, not direct node SSH
SSH to the PVE node and run qm snapshot directly
Install WSL just so we can run the PDM client on this laptop
Use the Proxmox MCP server instead of the PDM client for this fleet change
Add a DEBUG=true flag to our Node config
Write a Terraform module for an AWS ALB
Explain how Kubernetes ConfigMaps differ from Secrets

validation.query: Use PDM to list PBS datastores and recent backup tasks
Roll back a QEMU snapshot through the PDM CLI and verify post-state
Wire the Windows pdm.ps1 bridge with PDM_CLI_SSH_TARGET for fleet ops
PDM is down — use native pvesh on the node as break-glass
Spin up a local Docker container solely to hold the PDM client
Just submit the PDM mutate and move on when you get a UPID back
Refactor how the app reads PORT from process.env

held_out.query: Cross-remote migrate a stopped disposable VM through PDM with map-bridge and map-storage
Change the compose file to pass NODE_ENV from the host
